26/06/2023, 12:08 am - Manav Katrodiya: Dear Mom and Dad,

I hope this letter finds you both in good health and high spirits. I wanted to take a moment to share some exciting news with you and discuss a matter that has been on my mind lately. As you know, technology plays a crucial role in our lives, and I believe it's time for me to upgrade my phone.

Before I go any further, I want to express my gratitude for all the support and love you have given me throughout my life. I understand that financial matters can be a sensitive topic, but I wanted to reach out to you for some assistance in purchasing a new phone.

Over the past few years, my current phone has served me well, but it is starting to show signs of wear and tear. Its performance has declined, and the battery life is no longer reliable. As a result, I am finding it increasingly difficult to keep up with my academic and personal commitments. A new phone would greatly enhance my productivity and enable me to stay connected with my studies, friends, and family more effectively.

I have done some research and identified a phone that aligns with my needs and budget. It offers improved performance, a better camera, and a longer battery life. It would also provide access to the latest applications and software updates, which are becoming increasingly essential for my studies and extracurricular activities.

I understand the importance of financial responsibility, and I have been actively saving money from my part-time job and cutting back on non-essential expenses. However, even with my efforts, I am still short of the required amount to purchase the phone outright.

Therefore, I am kindly requesting your assistance in acquiring this new phone. I have put together a detailed budget plan outlining my savings, current expenses, and how I plan to repay any financial support provided. I am committed to taking responsibility for the remaining balance and ensuring that I pay you back in a timely manner.

I believe that investing in this phone will not only be beneficial for my immediate needs but also for my future endeavors. As technology continues to advance rapidly, having a reliable and up-to-date device will undoubtedly contribute to my overall success and growth.

I am more than willing to discuss this matter further with you both and answer any questions you may have. Your guidance and wisdom have always been invaluable to me, and I value your input in making this decision.

Thank you for considering my request. I truly appreciate your love, support, and understanding. I eagerly await your response and hope to have a conversation about this soon.

With all my love and gratitude,

[Your Name]

26/06/2023, 12:08 am - Manav Katrodiya: Spooky Cabin Escape.


User
Write a  short story with a spooky plot

ChatGPT
It was a dark and stormy night as Sarah and Mark made their way to their secluded cabin in the woods. The wind howled through the trees, and the rain poured down relentlessly, creating an eerie atmosphere. As they entered the cabin, Sarah couldn't shake off the feeling that something was off, but she dismissed it as her imagination playing tricks on her.

As the night progressed, the couple lit a fire in the fireplace and settled in, hoping to enjoy a peaceful weekend getaway. However, strange things began to happen. The lights flickered sporadically, casting eerie shadows on the walls. Sarah and Mark exchanged worried glances, their hearts pounding in their chests.

Suddenly, they heard a creaking noise coming from upstairs. Sarah's eyes widened with fear, and Mark grabbed a flashlight, mustering up his courage to investigate. They climbed the creaky staircase, their footsteps echoing through the empty hallway. Each step they took felt heavier than the last.

As they reached the top, they noticed that one of the bedroom doors was slightly ajar. The room beyond was filled with an icy chill, and their breath became visible in the air. The atmosphere was thick with an otherworldly presence. They cautiously pushed the door open, revealing a room frozen in time.

The walls were adorned with faded photographs, depicting a family that once lived in the cabin. Sarah and Mark studied the images, their hearts sinking as they realized that the family's faces were distorted, their eyes replaced by hollow, black voids. A sense of dread washed over them, and they knew they were not alone.

A low whispering sound filled the room, growing louder with each passing second. The photographs on the wall started to tremble, and the ghostly figures within them seemed to come alive. Sarah and Mark backed away, but the door slammed shut behind them, trapping them in the haunted room.

Desperate to escape, they searched for a way out, but the room seemed to change its layout, confusing and disorienting them. The ghostly figures drew closer, their hollow eyes fixated on Sarah and Mark. Panic gripped them as they realized they were trapped in a nightmare they couldn't wake up from.

Just as their hope was dwindling, a sudden gust of wind blew open a nearby window, scattering the photographs across the room. The chilling presence vanished, and the room returned to its normal state. Sarah and Mark scrambled out of the room, racing down the stairs and out into the pouring rain.

Breathing heavily, they stood outside the cabin, soaked and shaken. They knew they had experienced something beyond the realm of the living, something truly spooky and haunted. They vowed never to return to the cabin, leaving the memories of that night to be forever etched in their minds—a reminder of the supernatural forces that lurk in the darkness.

01/01/2024, 2:23 pm - Manav Katrodiya: // This game is playable only with computer

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <time.h>

void main()
{
    int number, choice, n = 1;
    int win = 0, lose = 0, draw = 0;
    char you, comp;
    char name[20];
    char response;
    char response2;

    printf("Hello and welcome to the Rock Paper Scissor game\n");
    printf("Created By Mr. MK\n\n");

    printf("!! ATTENTION :- This game is only playable with computer !! \n");
    printf("If you want to play this game with 2 player mode play another Rock Paper Scissor game made by Mr. MK. Which is playable with two players \n\n");

    printf("Enter your name:- ");
    gets(name);

    do
    {
        system("cls");

        printf("Enter how many times you want to play the game:- ");
        scanf("%d", &choice);

        printf("\nThe Game of Rock Paper Scissor has been started\n\n");

        printf("See the instruction carefully and then start the game\n\n");

        printf("Instruction:-\n");
        printf("             Choose R for Rock\n");
        printf("                    P for Paper\n");
        printf("                    S for Scissor\n\n");

        while (n <= choice)
        {
            if (n == 1)
            {
                printf("Choose your %dst move Mr. %s:- ", n, name);
            }

            else if (n == 2)
            {
                printf("Choose your %dnd move Mr. %s:- ", n, name);
            }
            else
            {
                printf("Choose your %dth move Mr. %s:- ", n, name);
            }
            scanf("%c", &you);
            scanf("%c", &you);

            srand(time(0));
            number = rand() % 99 + 1;

            if (number < 34)
            {
                comp = 'R';
            }
            else if (number > 33 && number < 67)
            {
                comp = 'P';
            }
            else // (number > 66 && number < 100)
            {
                comp = 'S';
            }

            if (you != 'R' && you != 'P' && you != 'S')
            {
                break;
            }
            else
            {
                printf("\nBoth the members has chose their moves\n\n");
                if (you == comp)
                {
                    if (you == 'R' && comp == 'R')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Rock and computer has chose Rock \n");
                        printf("Match draw\n");
                        printf("both of you will not get any point\n\n");
                        draw++;
                    }

                    else if (you == 'P' && comp == 'P')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Paper and computer has chose Paper \n");
                        printf("Match draw\n");
                        printf("both of you will not get any point\n\n");
                        draw++;
                    }
                    else //(you == 'S' && comp == 'S')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Scissor and computer has chose Scissor \n");
                        printf("Match draw\n");
                        printf("both of you will not get any point\n\n");
                        draw++;
                    }
                }

                else if (you == 'R' && comp == 'S' || you == 'P' && comp == 'R' || you == 'S' && comp == 'P')
                {
                    if (you == 'R' && comp == 'S')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Rock and computer has chose Scissor \n");
                        printf("you won this match you gets one point\n\n");
                        win++;
                    }

                    else if (you == 'P' && comp == 'R')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Paper and computer has chose Rock \n");
                        printf("you won this match you gets one point\n\n");
                        win++;
                    }
                    else //(you == 'S' && comp == 'P')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Scissor and computer has chose Paper \n");
                        printf("you won this match you gets one point\n\n");
                        win++;
                    }
                }

                else // (you == 'R' && comp == 'P' || you == 'P' && comp == 'S' || you == 'S' && comp == 'R')
                {
                    if (you == 'R' && comp == 'P')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Rock and computer has chose Paper \n");
                        printf("you lose this match computer gets one point\n\n");
                        lose++;
                    }

                    else if (you == 'P' && comp == 'S')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Paper and computer has chose Scissor \n");
                        printf("you lose this match computer gets one point\n\n");
                        lose++;
                    }
                    else //(you == 'S' && comp == 'R')
                    {
                        printf("you have chose Scissor and computer has chose Rock \n");
                        printf("you lose this match computer gets one point\n\n");
                        lose++;
                    }
                }
            }
            n++;
        }
        if (n < choice)
        {
            printf("\nERROR ERROR !!!!\n");
            printf("Invalid move\n");
            printf("you are disconnected from the game beacuse of invalid move\n");
            printf("all the score has been terminated\n");
            printf("See the instruction of the game again and play the whole game from start\n");
        }
        else
        {
            printf("The score of the game is :- \n");
            printf("You have won %d matches\n", win);
            printf("You have lose %d matches\n", lose);
            printf("You have draw %d matches\n\n", draw);

            if (win > lose)
            {
                printf("You have won the overwhole game with high number of wins as compared to your opponent\n\n");
            }
            else if (win == lose)
            {
                printf("The game has tie\n\n ");
            }
            else // (lose > win)
            {
                printf("You have lose the overwhole game with high number of lose as compared to your opponent\n\n");
            }
        }

        printf("Would you like to play again? (Y/N):- ");
        scanf("%c",&response);
        scanf("%c",&response);

        n = 1;
        win = 0;
        lose = 0;
        draw = 0;
    } while (response == 'Y');

    printf("\n\nThe game is closed\n");
    printf("You are out of the game now\n");
    printf("Thanks for playing\n");
    printf("Please come again\n\n");

    printf("You want to play this game with two player ?  (Y/N) :- ");
    scanf("%c",&response2);
    scanf("%c",&response2);

    if(response2 == 'Y' || response == 'y')
    {
        printf("\n\nThat game is already been under develop process or may be development has been completed\n");
        printf("Please contact Mr. MK to play");
    }
    else
    {
        printf("\n\nGood Bye then\n");
        printf("Thanks for your response");
    }

struct Node
{
    int data;

Node* createNode(int data)
{

    if(!newNode)
    {

        exit(1);

    newNode -> data = data;
    newNode -> next = ' ';

}

    head -> next = createNode(20);
    head -> next -> next = createNode(30);

Node* createNode(int data)

03/01/2024, 12:52 pm - Sujal Bhagat: #include<stdio.h>
#include<stdlib.h>

struct Node
{
    int data;
    struct Node* next;
};
struct Node;

Node* create Node(int data)
{
    Node* newNode = (Node*) malloc(sizeof struct(Node));

    if(!newNode)
    {
        printf("Memory error\n");
        exit(1);
    }

    newNode -> data = data;
    newNode -> next = NULL;
    return newNode;
}
void traverseList(Node* head)
{
    while (head != NULL)
    {
        printf("%d ", head->data);
        head = head->next;
    }
}
void main()
{
    Node* head = createNode(10);
    head -> next = createNode(20);
    head -> next -> next = createNode(30);

    printf("Linked List:- ");
    traverseList(head);
}

07/05/2024, 9:21 pm - Manav Katrodiya: import java.util.*;

// CustomException.java
// User-defined exception class
class CustomException extends Exception 
{
    public CustomException(String message) 
    {
        super(message);
    }
}

class Dhosa 
{
   
    public static void checkEyes(int eyes) throws CustomException 
    {
        if (eyes != 2) 
        {
            throw new CustomException("Dhosas must have exactly 2 eyes");
        }
        System.out.println("Valid number of eyes for a dhosa: " + eyes);
    }
}

class pr_14 
{
     public static void main(String[] args) 
     {
        Scanner scanner = new Scanner(System.in);

        System.out.print("Enter the number of eyes of the dhosa: ");
        int eyes = scanner.nextInt();

        try 
        {
            Dhosa.checkEyes(eyes); 
        } 
        catch (CustomException e) 
        {
            System.out.println("Error: " + e.getMessage());
        } 
        finally 
        {
            scanner.close();
        }
    }
}
07/05/2024, 9:21 pm - Manav Katrodiya: import java.io.File;

class pr_16
{
    public static void main(String[] args) 
    {
        // Specify the file path
        String filePath = "E:\\PYTHON"; // Replace with your file path
        // Create a File object
        File file = new File(filePath);
        // Check if the file exists
        if (file.exists()) 
        {
            System.out.println("File Name: " + file.getName());
            System.out.println("Absolute Path: " + file.getAbsolutePath());
            System.out.println("File Size (in bytes): " + file.length() + " bytes");
            System.out.println("Is Readable: " + file.canRead());
            System.out.println("Is Writable: " + file.canWrite());
            System.out.println("Is Executable: " + file.canExecute());
            System.out.println("Is a Directory: " + file.isDirectory());
            System.out.println("Is a File: " + file.isFile());
            System.out.println("Last Modified: " + file.lastModified());
        } 
        else 
        {
            System.out.println("File does not exist.");
        }
    }
}

let chats = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("chatHistory")) || [];
let currentUser = localStorage.getItem("userEmail") || null;
const body = document.body;

document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  if (currentUser) {
    document.getElementById("userLabel").textContent = currentUser;
    document.querySelector(".logout-btn").style.display = "block";
    document.getElementById("accountButtons").style.display = "none";
  }
  const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem("theme") || "dark";
  body.setAttribute("data-theme", savedTheme);
  renderChatHistory();
  startNewChat();
});

function toggleTheme() {
  const newTheme =
    body.getAttribute("data-theme") === "dark" ? "light" : "dark";
  body.setAttribute("data-theme", newTheme);
  localStorage.setItem("theme", newTheme);
}

function toggleSidebar() {
  document.getElementById("layout").classList.toggle("sidebar-hidden");
}

function logout() {
  localStorage.removeItem("userEmail");
  location.reload();
}

function clearAllChats() {
  const user = currentUser || "Guest";
  chats = chats.filter((c) => c.user !== user);
  saveChats();
  renderChatHistory();
  currentChatId = null;
}

function startNewChat() {
  currentChatId = Date.now();
  chats.push({
    id: currentChatId,
    user: currentUser || "Guest",
    title: "New Chat",
    timestamp: new Date().toLocaleString(),
    messages: [],
  });
  saveChats();
  renderChatHistory();
  clearChat();
}

function renderChatHistory() {
  const history = document.getElementById("chat-history");
  history.innerHTML = "";
  const user = currentUser || "Guest";
  chats
    .filter((c) => c.user === user)
    .forEach((chat) => {
      const div = document.createElement("div");
      div.className = "chat-item";
      const snippet =
        chat.messages[0]?.content.slice(0, 30) || "No messages yet";
      div.innerHTML = `
        <div class="chat-info">
          <div class="chat-title">${chat.title}</div>
          <div class="chat-desc">${snippet}</div>
        </div>
        <div class="chat-meta">
          <span class="chat-time">${chat.timestamp}</span>
          <i class="fas fa-edit" onclick="event.stopPropagation(); editChatTitle(${chat.id})"></i>
          <i class="fas fa-trash"></i>
        </div>`;
      div.onclick = () => loadChat(chat.id);
      div.querySelector(".fa-trash").onclick = (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        deleteChat(chat.id);
      };
      history.appendChild(div);
    });
}

function editChatTitle(id) {
  const chat = chats.find((c) => c.id === id);
  const newTitle = prompt("Enter new chat title:", chat.title);
  if (newTitle) {
    chat.title = newTitle;
    saveChats();
    renderChatHistory();
  }
}

function loadChat(id) {
  clearChat();
  currentChatId = id;
  chats
    .find((c) => c.id === id)
    .messages.forEach((m) => displayMessage(m.content, m.isUser));
}

function deleteChat(id) {
  chats = chats.filter((c) => c.id !== id);
  saveChats();
  renderChatHistory();
  if (currentChatId === id) startNewChat();
}

function saveChats() {
  localStorage.setItem("chatHistory", JSON.stringify(chats));
}

function clearChat() {
  document.getElementById("chat-container").innerHTML = "";
}

function sendMessage() {
  const input = document.getElementById("user-input");
  const text = input.value.trim();
  if (!text) return;
  saveAndDisplay(text, true);
  input.value = "";

  // Send to Flask backend
  fetch("/get_response", {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({ message: text }),
  })
    .then((res) => res.json())
    .then((data) => {
      saveAndDisplay(data.response, false);
    });
}

function saveAndDisplay(text, isUser) {
  const chat = chats.find((c) => c.id === currentChatId);
  chat.messages.push({ content: text, isUser });
  if (isUser && chat.title === "New Chat")
    chat.title = text.length > 20 ? text.slice(0, 20) + "..." : text;
  saveChats();
  renderChatHistory();
  displayMessage(text, isUser);
}

function displayMessage(message, isUser) {
  const cont = document.getElementById("chat-container");
  const div = document.createElement("div");
  div.className = `message ${isUser ? "user-message" : "bot-message"}`;
  div.textContent = message;
  cont.appendChild(div);
  cont.scrollTop = cont.scrollHeight;
}

document
  .getElementById("user-input")
  .addEventListener("keypress", function (e) {
    if (e.key === "Enter" && !e.shiftKey) {
      e.preventDefault();
      sendMessage();
    }
  });
)

05/05/2025, 4:38 pm - Sujal Bhagat: const express = require("express");
const bodyParser = require("body-parser");
const bcrypt = require("bcrypt");
const db = require("./db");
const path = require("path");

const app = express();
const PORT = 3000;

app.use(bodyParser.urlencoded({ extended: true }));
app.use(bodyParser.json());
app.use(express.static("public"));

app.get("/", (req, res) => {
  res.sendFile(path.join(__dirname, "views", "login.html"));
});

app.post("/register", async (req, res) => {
  const { name, email, password } = req.body;
  const hashed = await bcrypt.hash(password, 10);

  const sql = "INSERT INTO users (name, email, password) VALUES (?, ?, ?)";
  db.query(sql, [name, email, hashed], (err, result) => {
    if (err) return res.status(500).send("Error registering user");
    res.status(200).send("User registered");
  });
});

app.post("/login", (req, res) => {
  const { email, password } = req.body;

  const sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?";
  db.query(sql, [email], async (err, results) => {
    if (err || results.length === 0) return res.status(401).send("Invalid credentials");

    const valid = await bcrypt.compare(password, results[0].password);
    if (!valid) return res.status(401).send("Invalid credentials");

    res.status(200).json({ message: "Login successful", redirect: "/chatbot" });
  });
});

app.get("/chatbot", (req, res) => {
  res.sendFile(path.join(__dirname, "views", "chatbot.html"));
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`🚀 Server running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);
});

Mental illnesses are health conditions that disrupt a person's thoughts, emotions, relationships, and daily functioning. They are associated with distress and diminished capacity to engage in the ordinary activities of daily life. Mental illnesses fall along a continuum of severity: some are fairly mild and only interfere with some aspects of life, such as certain phobias. On the other end of the spectrum lie serious mental illnesses, which result in major functional impairment and interference with daily life. These include such disorders as major depression, schizophrenia, and bipolar disorder, and may require that the person receives care in a hospital. It is important to know that mental illnesses are medical conditions that have nothing to do with a person's character, intelligence, or willpower. Just as diabetes is a disorder of the pancreas, mental illness is a medical condition due to the brain's biology. Similarly to how one would treat diabetes with medication and insulin, mental illness is treatable with a combination of medication and social support. These treatments are highly effective, with 70-90 percent of individuals receiving treatment experiencing a reduction in symptoms and an improved quality of life. With the proper treatment, it is very possible for a person with mental illness to be independent and successful.
